symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe colicky left loin-to-groin pain, patient unable to stay still

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky unilateral loin-to-groin pain with restlessness is the classic presentation of an obstructing ureteric stone.
Mechanical back pain is positional and eased by stillness; colicky pain with groin radiation and restlessness argues against it.
Loin pain overlaps, but colicky restlessness favours obstruction; infection needs fever/rigors and urinalysis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea and repeated vomiting, unable to tolerate oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflex vomiting is common with the visceral pain of ureteric obstruction and, importantly, defeats oral analgesia/hydration.
Hypercalcaemia itself causes nausea/vomiting and can compound dehydration.
PTH-driven osteoclastic bone resorption produces diffuse bone pain — the 'bones' of the hypercalcaemia tetrad.
Bone pain may also reflect metastatic bone disease; separated from primary hyperparathyroidism by the PTH level.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic severe constipation refractory to laxatives

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypercalcaemia reduces gastrointestinal smooth-muscle motility — the 'abdominal groans' of the tetrad.
Hypercalcaemia impairs renal concentrating ability (nephrogenic diabetes insipidus), causing polyuria and compensatory polydipsia.
Polyuria/polydipsia is non-specific; glucose should be checked, but the wider constellation points to hypercalcaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic lethargy and generalised muscle weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypercalcaemia depresses neuromuscular and CNS function — the 'psychiatric moans' and weakness of the tetrad.
CVA tenderness localises the obstructed/inflamed upper tract — a clinician examination finding the AI cannot elicit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Left lower quadrant tenderness with diminished bowel sounds, no rebound/guarding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Referred tenderness and ileus from severe visceral pain; absence of peritonism argues against a surgical abdomen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse bony tenderness over the anterior tibiae and forearms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable bony tenderness reflects PTH-driven metabolic bone disease — a clinician exam finding, sealed from telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated (hypercalcaemia) — anticipated pending formal metabolic panel; not obtainable via telehealth
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The underlying hypercalcaemia is missed and the patient is treated as an isolated stone.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypercalcaemia is the entry point for the diagnosis; the value is anticipated but must be measured, never assumed or fabricated.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated or inappropriately normal in the face of hypercalcaemia — anticipated, not measurable via telehealth
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot distinguish primary hyperparathyroidism from PTH-independent (malignancy) hypercalcaemia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An elevated/inappropriately normal PTH with concurrent hypercalcaemia is diagnostic of primary hyperparathyroidism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-contrast CT KUB showing an obstructing ureteric calculus

investigation_details.expected_result: Left ureteric calculus with proximal dilatation — anticipated, not obtainable via telehealth
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Stone size, site and degree of obstruction unknown; obstructed infected kidney cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT KUB is the confirmatory imaging for a ureteric stone and its degree of obstruction.
Absence of fever/rigors makes an established obstructed infected kidney less likely now, but self-assessed temperature is unreliable and must be safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal femoral pulses, no pulsatile abdominal mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact femoral pulses and no pulsatile mass reduce the likelihood of AAA — a clinician exam finding the AI cannot obtain, so AAA cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypertension and tachycardia (BP 155/95 mmHg, HR 105 bpm)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain and distress readily explain the tachycardia and raised BP; these are clinician-measured values, sealed. The AI infers distress from the history, not a measured number.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The co-occurrence of a calcium stone, diffuse bone pain, refractory constipation, polyuria/polydipsia and lethargy is the classic symptomatic-hypercalcaemia syndrome and should trigger urgent calcium + PTH.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Entry test for the underlying hypercalcaemia; must be measured, never assumed.
Confirms primary hyperparathyroidism and separates it from malignancy-associated (PTH-independent) hypercalcaemia.
Confirms the stone, its site and the degree of obstruction, and helps exclude an obstructed infected kidney.
Assesses acute kidney injury from obstruction/dehydration and baseline renal function before any NSAID.
Screens for urinary infection — critical given the obstructed-infected-kidney risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum phosphate, vitamin D and 24-hour urinary calcium
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Complete the metabolic work-up of hyperparathyroidism/hypercalcaemia once the acute episode is controlled.
Polyuria/polydipsia is non-specific; exclude diabetes mellitus as a contributor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Contrast CT / intravenous urogram as first-line
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Non-contrast CT KUB is the appropriate first-line imaging; contrast studies are not needed up front and add nephrotoxic risk in a dehydrated patient.
